Your Role

As Senior Oracle DBA at Bank J. Safra Sarasin you are responsible for operations, support as well as the continuous development of our Oracle environment globally and provide a high level storage service to the Bank's critical applications.

Your Responsibilities

  • Support operations with troubleshooting alongside the incident management
  • Implement change requests (RFC) in line with existing procedures and protocols
  • Optimize resources utilization by analyzing and proposing improvements considering the life-cycle management of applications hosted
  • Development and implementation of automated solutions
  • Collaboration in the continuous development of existing infrastructure and active support of migration projects
  • Recovery exercises execution and troubleshooting in the 2nd level support incl. on-call service

Your Profile

  • Higher education (university / FH) in Computer Science or any other relevant education
  • At least 7 years of practical experience in the field of database operations and engineering
  • Knowledge in Oracle databases and PostgreSQL is required
  • Experience with Ansible automation is a plus
  • Strong team player and ability to recognize synergies
  • Hands-on mentality and willingness to take responsibility
  • Strong work ethics, integrity and high level of accountability
  • Fluency in English; fluency in German is a plus

Your Benefits

  • Entrepreneurial spirit in a family owned bank
  • Central city office locations across Switzerland
  • Above average insurance coverage fully borne by the bank
  • Contribution to health insurance and meal allowance
  • Global growth strategy and stable environment
  • Competitive offering

About us

As a leading sustainable private bank, we offer dynamic and personalized services in investment advisory and asset management for private and institutional clients.Our success depends on the enthusiasm and commitment of our employees and we value each person with their unique technical expertise, professional qualifications and social skills.

We are successfully operating for more than 180 years across 30 locations worldwide and offer our employees the chance to be a part of our success.Our employees are our most valuable asset, we are proud of our unique culture that fosters entrepreneurship and is fueled by a high team spirit andthe positive attitude of each individual. Join our international team and be part of the next chapter of the J. Safra Sarasin Group.

Your Role

As AI Platform System Engineer at Bank J. Safra Sarasin you are strengthening our platform team as a professional and experienced engineer and provide technical engineering and operational infrastructure services to enterprise infrastructure for AI initiatives in close collaboration with several stakeholders.

Your Responsibilities

  • Design and evolve scalable AI infrastructure services optimized for GPU workloads
  • Implement and manage enterprise-grade AI platforms (e.g., Kubernetes, OpenShift) for model inference
  • Operate and maintain high-performance computing (HPC) clusters, including bare-metal and virtual GPU servers
  • Troubleshoot and resolve complex infrastructure incidents across hardware and software stacks
  • Support data science teams with infrastructure provisioning
  • Perform critical infrastructure tasks and participate in on-call rotations

Your Profile

  • University degree (university / FH) in Computer Science
  • 5 years of IT system administration experience, including 3+ years specializing in Linux (RHEL preferred) and strong scripting skills in Shell, Python, and Ansible
  • Deep expertise in GPU topologies and architectures (NVLink, PCIe switching) and container orchestration (Kubernetes, Terraform, ArgoCD). Initial hands-on experience in Prometheus, Grafana, and DCGM Exporter is required
  • Familiarity with ITIL methodologies, combined with a proactive, accountable work ethic. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, effective prioritization, and the ability to deliver results under pressure
  • Fluent in English; proficiency in German is a plus

Your Benefits

  • Entrepreneurial spirit in a family owned bank
  • Central city office locations across Switzerland
  • Above average insurance coverage fully borne by the bank
  • Contribution to health insurance and meal allowance
  • Global growth strategy and stable environment

Help our clients
successfully manage global challenges 

As a Senior Consultant in the Tech M&A cohort of our Deal
Transformation practice, you
will support corporate and private equity clients in understanding the
technology dimension of their most important transactions. Technology has
become one of the defining value drivers in M&A—it shapes deal theses,
influences valuation, creates integration and separation complexity, and
ultimately determines how value is protected and delivered after close. With AI
accelerating change across industries, you will help clients assess technology
opportunities, risks and disruption while supporting informed deal decisions
and long-term value creation.

How you will make an
impact 

  • Technology
    due diligence    : Assess technology landscapes, applications, infrastructure, cloud,
    data, cybersecurity, operating models and technology spend to identify
    transaction risks and opportunities.
  • Technology
    integrations and carve-outs    : Support technology separation and integration
    planning, including Day 1 readiness, TSA considerations, target-state design
    and implementation planning.
  • Technology
    strategy and value creation    : Analyse technology strategy, AI impact, operating
    model improvements and value creation opportunities across the transaction
    lifecycle.
  • Technology
    cost and synergy analysis    : Support technology cost assessments, synergy
    analysis and investment planning while working closely with financial and
    commercial deal teams.
  • Client
    delivery    : Translate complex technology findings into clear analyses and
    executive-ready recommendations while collaborating with multidisciplinary deal
    team

Who we are looking
for 

  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a strong interest in
    technology, M&A and value creation; curiosity for emerging
    technologies.
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving
    capabilities, with the ability to simplify complex technology topics.
  • Bachelor's or Master's degree from a leading
    university.
  • Minimum of 3 years of relevant experience in
    M&A, technology strategy, technology transformation, transaction
    advisory or a related industry role.
  • Strong acumen for project and stakeholder
    management with paired with high-quality skills in Excel, PowerPoint and
    executive storytelling. Fluency in English. German and/or French is desired.

Joining our team 

The Tech M&A team cohort within our Swiss
Deal Transformation practice advises clients on the technology dimension of Mergers & Acquisitions,
Divestitures, Carve-outs and Integrations. As part of Deloitte's end-to-end
M&A advisory offering, we work closely with colleagues from Transaction
Services, Valuations, Corporate Finance, Commercial, Strategy, Cyber, AI &
Data and broader Transformation teams to provide clients with an integrated,
value-driven approach to transactions.

You will gain exposure
to high-profile transactions across industries where technology increasingly
determines value, risk and execution success. Working alongside
multidisciplinary teams, you will help clients navigate complex technology
decisions throughout the deal lifecycle while developing deep expertise at the
intersection of technology and M&A.

Careers at Deloitte. Choose your impact. 

At Deloitte, your ideas create impact and spark meaningful change for our clients, people and society. Your unique expertise, background and perspective helps us find new ways into the most complex challenges so we can brighten the futures of those our work affects. At Deloitte, you can build a career that inspires and energises you. At Deloitte, you can choose your impact.

In Switzerland, we provide industry-specific services in the areas of Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal, Strategy, Risk & Transactions Advisory, and Technology & Transformation. With close to 3,000 employees at six locations, we serve companies and organisations of all sizes in all industry sectors. We offer meaningful and challenging work, an inclusive and supportive environment where you can be your best self and where you will never stop growing. You will benefit from flexible and hybrid working conditions, wellbeing and parental programmes as well as many opportunities to connect, collaborate and learn. 

Join us and become part of a global network of like-minded people dedicated to making a difference, whether by addressing climate change or improving 100 million futures by 2030. Want to know more about opportunities at Deloitte? Visit www.deloitte.com/ch/careers

Hauptaufgaben

  • Durchführung von ATP/CTP-Analysen zur Sicherstellung realistischer Liefertermine unter Berücksichtigung von Materialverfügbarkeit, Kapazitäten und Stücklisten
  • Erstellung, Steuerung und Stabilisierung des Master Schedules für definierte Produktlinien
  • Freigabe und Terminierung von Fertigungsaufträgen in Abstimmung mit Produktion und Kundenanforderungen
  • Proaktive Identifikation, Analyse und Behebung von Engpässen entlang der Supply Chain
  • Durchführung von Root Cause Analysen sowie Abstimmung von Massnahmen mit relevanten Schnittstellen
  • Aktives Backlog-Management sowie Überwachung und Analyse relevanter KPIs (z. B. OTD, Durchlaufzeiten)
  • Sicherstellung der Datenqualität von Planungsparametern sowie Nutzung von Excel und Power BI für datenbasierte Entscheidungen

  • Höhere technische oder kaufmännische Ausbildung (HF, FH oder Bachelor) mit Schwerpunkt Supply Chain Management, Produktionsplanung oder Logistik
  • Mindestens 3 Jahre Berufserfahrung in einer vergleichbaren Funktion (z. B. Master Scheduler, Produktionsplaner), idealerweise im HMLV-Umfeld
  • Ausgeprägte analytische und konzeptionelle Fähigkeiten sowie strukturierte Problemlösungsansätze
  • Fundiertes technisches Verständnis für mechanische und elektromechanische Produkte
  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse in ERP-Systemen (MS Dynamics 365 F&O von Vorteil) sowie in MRP-Prozessen
  • Fundiertes Wissen in der Produktionsplanung (ATP/CTP, Engpassmanagement, Master Scheduling, Backlog-Steuerung)
  • Erfahrung im Umgang mit Planungsparametern (z. B. Lead Times, Sicherheitsbestände, Losgrössen)
  • Selbstständige, belastbare und durchsetzungsstarke Persönlichkeit mit ausgeprägten Kommunikations- und Organisationsfähigkeiten
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- und gute Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift

Nächster Schritt: Project Leader bei BDO

Hast du Lust, uns mit deiner dynamischen und offenen Persönlichkeit bei unseren Projekten zu unterstützen?
Bei uns kannst du deine Erfahrung im Project Management einbringen und dich - sowie uns - weiterentwickeln.
 

IT Project Leader (w/m/d) 60-100%

Das bewegst du

  • Du gestaltest die digitale und organisatorische Weiterentwicklung von BDO aktiv mit und bringst deine Ideen dort ein, wo Menschen, Prozesse und Technologie zusammenwirken.

  • Du hinterfragst Bestehendes konstruktiv, erkennst Potenziale und hilfst mit, Innovation, Effizienz und Kundennähe bei BDO weiterzuentwickeln. 

Damit gelingt es dir

  • Du verfügst über einen Abschluss auf Stufe HF, FH oder Universität in Wirtschaftsinformatik oder einer vergleichbaren Fachrichtung.

  • Zertifikate wie IPMA, IREB, CPAB, Scrum Master, Product Owner, SaFe oder ITIL sind von Vorteil
  • Du hast eine hohe IT-Affinität und ein breites technisches Verständnis, idealerweise im Microsoft-Umfeld
  • Du bist eine kommunikative, kreative und teamorientierte Persönlichkeit mit Hands-on-Mentalität

Wir von Visana gestalten die Zukunft des Gesundheitswesens - mit Leidenschaft und Innovation. Du willst etwas bewirken? Dann bist du bei uns richtig! Visana ist mit 922'000 Privat- und 16'300 Unternehmenskundinnen und -kunden ein führender Schweizer Allbranchenversicherer mit Sitz in Bern. Wir begleiten unsere Kundinnen und Kunden in den unterschiedlichsten Lebenssituationen - von Gesundheit und Vorsorge über Wohnen und Mobilität bis hin zu individuellen Lösungen für Unternehmen. Mach den nächsten Karriereschritt - unser Team freut sich auf dich!

Das CIO Office ist der strategische Dreh- und Angelpunkt der CSS-IT. Hier laufen Strategie, Transformation und operative Steuerung zusammen. Wir schaffen Transparenz, setzen Prioritäten und sorgen dafür, dass Investitionen und Innovationen wirksam umgesetzt werden.
Gemeinsam mit über 350 Fachprofis koordinieren wir das IT-Ökosystem, entwickeln die IT- und Sourcing-Strategie weiter, verantworten Budget & Controlling, treiben IT Risk Management und Governance voran und unterstützen Kommunikation, Events sowie Projekte. Als CIO Office Manager/in agierst du als IT Inhouse Consultant, arbeitest eng mit dem CIO sowie dem IT Leadership Team zusammen und berichtest an die Leiterin des CIO Office. Hast du Lust mit viel Eigenverantwortung und Gestaltungsspielraum die digitale Zukunft der CSS mitzugestalten? Dann freuen wir uns auf dich.

CIO Office Manager/in 80-100%

So sieht dein Alltag aus

  • Zum Herzstück: Du unterstützt den CIO und das IT Leadership Team bei der Planung und Umsetzung strategischer Initiativen und sorgst dafür, dass Themen strukturiert vorangetrieben werden. 
  • Du übernimmst die Programmsteuerung bereichsübergreifender IT-Projekte und Transformationsinitiativen. Dabei führst du Initiativen fachlich - ohne disziplinarische Führungsverantwortung -, koordinierst Stakeholder, steuerst Abhängigkeiten und Ressourcen und sorgst für eine termingerechte Umsetzung. 
  • Du analysierst und strukturierst strategische Fragestellungen, entwickelst fundierte Entscheidungsgrundlagen und bereitest komplexe Themen adressatengerecht in Form von professionellen Entscheidungsvorlagen und Präsentationen für Sitzungen oder Workshops verschiedener Management-Ebenen auf. 
  • Workshops und Meetings planst und moderierst du souverän und erstellst dazu passende Entscheidungsunterlagen und Präsentationen.
  • Bei Bedarf unterstützt du Budgetplanung, IT-Controlling und Finanzreporting und übernimmst diese Aufgaben in Stellvertretung eigenständig.

Das bringst du mit

  • Das Wichtigste zuerst: Du verfügst über mehrjährige Erfahrung in der Steuerung komplexer IT-Projekte, Programme oder Transformationsinitiativen in einem agilen Umfeld. Ein Plus, wenn du ebenso Erfahrung in der Strategieberatung oder Managementberatung sammeln konntest. 
  • Du bringst ein abgeschlossenes Studium in IT, Wirtschaft, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder einem verwandten Bereich mit - wir schätzen deine individuelle Karrieregeschichte. 
  • Dank deiner fundierten Projektmanagement-Kenntnisse sowie deiner ausgeprägten Organisations- und Priorisierungsfähigkeiten führst du komplexe Transformations- oder IT-Programme mit Durchblick und Drive zum Ziel. 
  • Du erstellst eigenständig Präsentationen auf Executive-Niveau. Mit exzellenten PowerPoint-Kenntnissen, einem ausgeprägten Gespür für Storytelling und einem hohen Qualitätsanspruch bereitest du komplexe Themen verständlich, visuell überzeugend und entscheidungsreif auf. 
  • Workshops moderierst du souverän, bewegst dich sicher auf C-Level und packst Herausforderungen mit Begeisterung an.
  • Du überzeugst durch strategisches, analytisches und unternehmerisches Denken und behältst auch in einem dynamischen Umfeld den Überblick.

job_role: Systems and Core Assets Product Line Architect 80-100%
job_description:
Youtube Video (Arbeiten bei Siemens Schweiz: Transform the everyday with us)

Together with our customers, we combine the real and digital worlds

With technology, software and services for smart buildings, we transform the everyday for everyone. By combining the real and digital worlds, we empower our customers to reach their decarbonization goals, lower their operating costs and create world-class occupant experiences for their people. It's not just about improving buildings - it's about creating efficient, safe, adaptable and sustainable environments, which improve the way the world lives and works.

The digital transformation of buildings is a fast-changing, dynamic sector, and it needs more than just great technology - it needs great people, too. We know a business only thrives if its people are thriving, so at Siemens we put our people first.

Within our buildings business, our diverse global team will support you through your career and challenge you to grow in new and exciting ways, as we transform the world's infrastructure.

Our shared journey could take you anywhere...where would you like to go?

As Systems and Core Assets Product Line Architect, you are responsible for defining and evolving system architecture for building automation systems. You will sharpen the technical vision, defining the strategy for developing common reusable core assets, and evaluating emerging technologies to realize Human-Centric Autonomous Buildings. This role requires operational leadership to deliver high quality and reusable core assets. You will be working with different stakeholders like architects, product managers, project managers and developers. We will be happy to introduce you to our exciting environment! 

Location: this role will be based in Zug/Switzerland. Please note that qualified candidates need to be legally eligible for work authorization in Switzerland.

Your new responsibilities

  • Define the architectural runway, technology roadmap, cybersecurity, and ensure total cost of ownership and end-to-end architectural consistency across different products and different R&D sites
  • Responsible for identifying, shaping, maintaining and driving the strategy and implementation of Core Assets to be reused by multiple product and system offerings
  • Participating in cybersecurity and standardization effort to identify opportunities in the organization where core assets representing such technology should be created, maintained and reused
  • Lead diverse and global teams, working with internal and external stakeholders and partners, to create holistic solutions for building automation
  • Evaluate organizational and market requirements, recommend cutting-edge technologies, and design products to optimize system performance, simplify workflows and enable business outcomes
  • Translate non-functional requirements like scalability, interoperability, and reliability into iterative, risk-based development processes, ensuring alignment with the evolving needs of Autonomous Buildings
  • Guide business strategies in HVAC and building automation towards a modular architecture that delivers interoperability across products for seamless operation

Your talents and experience

  • Bachelor's, or master's degree in engineering or technology, preferably electrical & electronic engineering, information technology, computer science or similar education
  • At least five years of experience in systems engineering, systems architecture, and hands-on experience in software development
  • Very good knowledge of Web of Things (WoT) and semantic data schemas and ontologies, e.g. Brick, Haystack
  • Full-stack knowhow of system architecture and technologies from field devices to PC-based tooling, edge and cloud systems and cybersecurity
  • Extensive experience in commonality and variability management in complex systems and software product line engineering, such as domain engineering, feature modeling and software factory methods
  • Experience in establishing systems architecture in products and systems development based on agile and SAFe development processes and methods
  • Strong skills in stakeholder management, cross-culture collaboration and leadership
  • Preferably you bring domain knowledge in Building Automation, HVAC, Lighting, Shading
  • Proficient communication and presentation abilities in English. German and any additional language is a plus

Employee benefits

  • 2-3 days per week of mobile working is standard
  • Mobility allowance
  • Varied further training opportunities
  • Access to employee share programs
  • Further information about employee benefits can be found here

Our corporate culture - your new working environment

The culture at Siemens Switzerland is more than just a working environment - it is at the heart of our day-to-day interactions and the key to our success. Siemens Switzerland offers a future-oriented working environment characterized by innovation, sustainability and personal development. We are characterized by personal responsibility, equal opportunities and diversity, to which every employee can contribute authentically. Flexible working models and remote working are a reality for us and enable our employees to achieve a good work-life balance. Learn more and find out if you are a good match with Siemens by taking our culture quiz! 

News and stories

See for yourself how innovative technology can change the present. If you want to find out more about what projects can be implemented using Siemens technology, here are some current examples.

Frequently asked questions and contact information

Here you will find a collection of frequently asked questions and a way to contact us directly.

I look forward to receiving your application.

Nathalie
Talent Acquisition Partner

Your way to Siemens - How we hire

job_role: PI Data Historian, Integration System Engineer (a) 80 - 100%
job_description:
Bachem ist ein börsenkotiertes, auf die Peptid-Chemie fokussiertes Technologie-Unternehmen mit einem umfassenden Leistungsangebot für die Pharma- und Biotechnologie-Industrie. Bachem ist spezialisiert auf die Entwicklung innovativer, effizienter Herstellungsverfahren und die zuverlässige Produktion Peptid-basierter pharmazeutischer Wirkstoffe. Ein umfangreicher Katalog von Biochemikalien für Forschungszwecke und exklusive Kundensynthesen ergänzen das Angebot. Mit Hauptsitz in der Schweiz sowie Niederlassungen in Europa und den USA ist das Unternehmen weltweit tätig und verfügt über die industrieweit grösste Erfahrung und Fachkompetenz. In der Zusammenarbeit mit ihren Kunden legt Bachem grössten Wert auf Qualität, Innovation und Partnerschaft.

PI Data Historian, Integration System Engineer (a) 80 - 100%

The Bachem Group is investing in a new planned site in Sisslerfeld in Northwestern Switzerland. This step is part of the long-term strategy to expand production capacity by the end of the decade. The new production site will play a central role in achieving these goals. The design phase of the project offers you unique opportunities to shape the future organization.

 

To drive our continuous organic growth, we are constantly looking for highly qualified professionals. To strengthen our team in Global IT, we are looking for a dedicated PI Data Historian, Integration System Engineer (a), workload 80 - 100%.

 

Your tasks:

  • Manage the full lifecycle of the AVEVA PI Data Historian System, including architecture, development, installation, commissioning, maintenance, upgrades, and decommissioning activities.
  • Support greenfield and brownfield manufacturing site projects, including deployment of PI infrastructure for new production facilities and expansion projects.
  • Lead PI System implementation activities during new manufacturing site start-up phases, including system design, validation, commissioning, and operational readiness.
  • Administer a 24/7 operational PI System, ensuring high availability, reliability, performance, and disaster recovery readiness.
  • Design, implement, and optimize historian solutions integrating OT equipment, PLCs, SCADA, DCS, MES, and enterprise IT systems.
  • Drive end-to-end IT/OT integration activities to enable seamless data flow between manufacturing equipment, automation systems, and business applications.
  • Collaborate closely with automation, infrastructure, cybersecurity, manufacturing, and digitalization teams to ensure compliant and scalable system integration.
  • Lead change, incident, and problem management processes in collaboration with IT/OT teams, cross-functional stakeholders, vendors, and end users.
    Troubleshoot and resolve complex issues related to industrial data collection, communication interfaces, data storage, synchronization, and retrieval.
  • Translate business and operational requirements into robust technical solutions aligned with manufacturing and digital transformation strategies.
    Design and configure complex PI tags, AF structures, event frames, and data models tailored to operational and business needs.
  • Develop and maintain custom interfaces, connectors, and scripts to enhance interoperability between OT equipment and enterprise IT platforms.
  • Support commissioning and qualification activities for connected manufacturing systems and ensure compliance with GMP and validation requirements.
  • Conduct system performance analysis and drive continuous improvement initiatives for historian and integration platforms.
  • Provide technical leadership and mentorship to junior engineers and project team members.
  • Collaborate with external vendors, system integrators, and engineering partners during project execution and operational support activities.
  • Create and maintain comprehensive GMP-compliant documentation, including design specifications, test protocols, validation records, SOPs, and lifecycle documents.
  • Act as Subject Matter Expert (SME) during audits, inspections, and assessments, ensuring compliance with internal standards, regulatory requirements, and data integrity principles.
     

Your profile:
 

  • Bachelor's degree in software engineering, Computer Science, Information Technology, Automation Engineering, or a related technical field.
  • 4 - 6 years of hands-on experience in PI Historian engineering, system integration, and technical project delivery within pharmaceutical manufacturing environments.
  • Proven hands-on experience with the AVEVA PI Data Historian System, including PI Server, PI AF, PI Vision, Interfaces, Connectors & Adaptors.
  • Strong project experience supporting new manufacturing site deployments, commissioning activities, or large-scale production expansion projects.
  • Demonstrated experience integrating OT equipment and industrial automation systems with enterprise IT infrastructure and applications.
  • Solid understanding of industrial communication protocols such as OPC DA/UA, modbus, MQTT, BACnet, or Ethernet/IP.
  • Strong background in managing and administering production-critical applications in regulated manufacturing environments.
  • Experience working with PLCs, SCADA, DCS, MES, manufacturing execution & integration platform architectures.
  • Familiarity with infrastructure technologies including Windows Server, virtualization, networking, Active Directory, backup/recovery, and cybersecurity principles.
  • Experience with Computerized System Validation (CSV), GMP compliance, and data integrity requirements within regulated industries.
  • Strong troubleshooting, analytical, and communication skills with the ability to work across IT and OT domains.
  • In-depth knowledge of industrial data integration, historian system architecture, and real-time data management.
  • Understanding of manufacturing processes, automation systems, and digital manufacturing initiatives.
  • Proficiency in scripting and automation tools (e.g., PowerShell, Python) for system administration and workflow automation.
  • Experience with data contextualization, visualization, reporting, and analytics platforms.
  • Ability to work effectively in cross-functional and global project environments.
  • Fluent in English; German language skills are highly desirable

Our offer

 

  • A dynamic and rapidly growing work environment with internal development opportunities
  • Flexible working hours with home office days and an option for obtaining additional vacation days through workload reduction
  • Employee development through numerous internal and external training opportunities
  • 60% coverage of pension fund contributions by Bachem as well as option for extra-mandatory pension provision with our Pension Plan Plus
  • Access to benefits platform with discounts from external partners

 

 

Would you like to drive innovation together with us?

We look forward to receiving your complete application documents via our application portal.

Learn more about the Bachem Group and get inspired by our exciting work environment at our new location in Stein!

About VAT Group

At VAT, we change the world with vacuum solutions. As the world's leading supplier of high-performance vacuum valves, we have been driving innovation for more than 60 years. With over 3,200 employees worldwide, we operate from our headquarters in Haag, Switzerland, with manufacturing sites in Switzerland, Malaysia and Romania, as well as sales and service hubs around the world. We guided by our passions integrity, teamwork, customer centricity, and innovation - always working together as #oneVAT.

Joining us means becoming part of a passionate, international team where your voice is heard, your ideas matter, and your career growth is supported.

Logistics Manager

 

Responsibilities:

 

Leadership & Team Management

 

  • Provide functional and disciplinary leadership to Team Leaders within the assigned logistics area.
  • Lead workforce planning activities, including staffing, recruitment, and resource allocation in line with budget targets.
  • Set performance objectives, provide regular feedback, and conduct performance evaluations.
  • Foster a high-performance culture through coaching, employee engagement, and talent development.
  • Ensure compliance with company policies, safety standards, and operational procedures.

 

Operational Excellence

 

  • Manage daily logistics operations to ensure smooth, efficient, and timely execution.
  • Monitor, analyze, and optimize operational KPIs related to productivity, quality, and service performance.
  • Act as Process Steward for the assigned logistics area, ensuring process compliance and continuous improvement.
  • Lead and support projects focused on operational excellence, process optimization, and organizational development.
  • Develop, maintain, and update Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and process documentation.

 

Quality & Cross-Functional Collaboration

 

  • Serve as the key interface between Logistics and internal stakeholders, including Procurement, Supply Chain, Quality, and Production.
  • Coordinate and resolve quality, material, and delivery-related issues and implement corrective actions when required.
  • Ensure inventory accuracy through effective inventory management and oversight of physical inventory counts.
  • Drive initiatives to improve service levels, operational quality, and customer satisfaction.

 

Performance, Safety & Compliance

 

  • Take ownership of quality, productivity, and safety performance within the area of responsibility.
  • Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements, internal standards, and audit expectations.
  • Support strategic and operational planning activities to achieve short- and long-term business objectives.

 

Qualifications:

 

  • Degree, apprenticeship, or professional qualification in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or a related field.
  • Additional logistics certification (e.g., Logistics Specialist or equivalent) is highly desirable.
  • Experience with logistics automation initiatives, such as automated storage and retrieval systems (ASRS), conveyor systems, automated guided vehicles (AGVs/AMRs), warehouse automation, or digital transformation projects within a logistics environment.
  • Proven leadership experience in logistics, warehousing, or supply chain operations.
  • Strong knowledge of warehouse management and ERP system, Microsoft Dynamics 365 F&O preferred
  • Advanced Excel skills and experience with KPI reporting and performance analysis.
  • Demonstrated ability to independently plan, manage, and execute projects.
  • Professional proficiency in German and English.
  • Strong leadership presence with the ability to motivate and develop teams.
  • Excellent organizational and prioritization skills.
  • Resilient and capable of performing in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
  • Strong communication, stakeholder management, and conflict resolution skills.
  • Results-oriented mindset with a continuous improvement focus.
  • Ability to make decisions and drive accountability across teams.

 

Suisse 9469 Haag Seelistrasse 1

Data Manager

NEXUS Personalized Health is the technology platform for personalized health at ETH Zurich. Since 2015, NEXUS has enabled and accelerated biomedical research by providing scientific expertise and tailored services in clinical bioinformatics, software engineering, biostatistics, high-throughput screening, and data management. Working at the interface between research and clinical application, NEXUS collaborates with researchers from ETH Zurich, hospitals, academia, and industry to develop innovative solutions that advance personalized medicine.

Project background

NEXUS supports two cutting-edge biomedical research projects that rely on robust and secure research data management. Data and metadata are collected from multiple laboratories, stored on the highly secure LeoMed platform, and consolidated within a LabKey installation to provide researchers with high-quality, well-organized datasets.

To cover a maternity leave, we are looking for a Data Manager for a fixed-term appointment of six months. In this role, you will support the day-to-day data management activities of ongoing research projects, ensuring data quality, consistency, and compliance while working closely with researchers.

This is a fixed-term position for 6 months, from September 2026 to February 2027.

Job description

As a Data Manager, you will support biomedical research projects by implementing and maintaining good data management practices. Your responsibilities include:

  • Collect, curate and organize research data and metadata from multiple laboratories.
  • Ensure data completeness, consistency and quality throughout the project lifecycle.
  • Maintain and update metadata within LabKey and related systems.
  • Write and maintain scripts (e.g. in Python or Bash) to parse, transform and validate research data.
  • Support data synchronization and secure storage on LeoMed.
  • Assist researchers with data management questions and documentation.
  • Help implement data governance and FAIR data principles within research projects.
  • Document data workflows, standards and project procedures.
  • Collaborate with software engineers and researchers to improve data management processes.

Profile

Required qualifications

  • MSc Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, Computer Science, Data Science, Life Sciences or a related field.
  • Experience working with biomedical or scientific research data.
  • Good scripting skills (preferably Python; Bash or similar languages are also welcome) for parsing, transforming and validating data.
  • Basic understanding of relational databases and SQL.
  • Familiarity with research data management concepts and metadata.
  • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
  • Very good written and spoken English.

Nice to have

  • Experience with LeoMed or other secure computing environments.
  • Experience using LabKey or similar laboratory information/data management systems.
  • Knowledge of FAIR data principles.
  • Experience working in collaborative biomedical research projects.

We offer

  • An opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge biomedical research at ETH Zurich.
  • Flexible working hours and the possibility to work partially from home.
  • A collegial, interdisciplinary and supportive team.
  • A structured onboarding and handover for this temporary position.
  • Modern office facilities at the Bio-Technopark Schlieren.
  • The opportunity to gain experience with state-of-the-art biomedical data infrastructures and collaborate with leading researchers.
  • Attractive employment conditions according to ETH Zurich regulations.
Working, teaching and research at ETH Zurich

We value diversity and sustainability

In line with our values, ETH Zurich encourages an inclusive culture. We promote equality of opportunity, value diversity and nurture a working and learning environment in which the rights and dignity of all our staff and students are respected. Visit our Equal Opportunities and Diversity website to find out how we ensure a fair and open environment that allows everyone to grow and flourish. Sustainability is a core value for us – we are consistently working towards a climate-neutral future.

Curious? So are we.

We look forward to receiving your online application with the following documents:

  • CV
  • Motivation Letter
  • Certificates

Further information about NEXUS Personalized Health can be found on our Website. Questions regarding the position should be directed to David Meyer (no applications).

Please note that we exclusively accept applications submitted through our online application portal. Applications via email or postal services will not be considered.

We would like to point out that the pre-selection is carried out by the responsible recruiters and not by artificial intelligence.

For recruitment services the GTC of ETH Zurich apply.

About ETH Zürich

ETH Zurich is one of the world’s leading universities specialising in
science and technology. We are renowned for our excellent education,
cutting-edge fundamental research and direct transfer of new knowledge
into society. Over 30,000 people from more than 120 countries find our
university to be a place that promotes independent thinking and an
environment that inspires excellence. Located in the heart of Europe,
yet forging connections all over the world, we work together to
develop solutions for the global challenges of today and tomorrow.

ICT System Spezialist:in (m/w/d)

Zur Unterstützung von Forschung und Lehre sind die Informatikdienste der ETH Zürich als Dienstleistungsorganisation in einer grossen und komplexen IT-Umgebung tätig. Wir bieten den Angehörigen der ETH Zürich hochwertige, flexible und skalierbare Informatik-Dienstleistungen an und unterstützen sie damit im Wettbewerb mit den besten Universitäten der Welt.

Die Sektion ID Customer Experience and Solutions (CxS) ist für den User-Support und das IT-Arbeitsplatzmanagement in Forschungsdepartementen, den zentralen administrativen Einheiten sowie bei ETH-nahen Einheiten zuständig. Zusätzlich verwaltet CxS die zentralen Computerarbeitsräume und betreibt die Multifunktionsdrucker an der ETH.

Stellenbeschreibung

Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams suchen wir per sofort oder nach Vereinbarung eine erfahrene und engagierte Persönlichkeit als ICT System Spezialist:in (m/w/d)

Gestalten und Bewegen

In dieser vielseitigen Position bist du für die Planung, Weiterentwicklung und den reibungslosen Betrieb unserer heterogenen IT-Infrastruktur verantwortlich. Du schlägst die Brücke zwischen modernster Technik und exzellenter Forschung.

  • System-Engineering: Du administrierst und optimierst unsere Client- Infrastruktur (Windows, Mac und Linux).
  • Support & Infrastruktur: Du stellst den Betrieb der IT-Arbeitsplätze sicher und leistest kompetenten Support.
  • Innovation durch Automatisierung: Mittels Scripting und moderner Tools automatisierst du Prozesse und entwickelst unsere IT-Services proaktiv weiter.
  • Consulting: Du unterstützt und berätst Mitarbeitende der ZO bei allen IT-Fragen.
  • Projekte: Neue Kundenanforderungen setzt du durch innovative Lösungen im Rahmen von Projekten um.

Profil

Fachverstand trifft Teamgeist

Du arbeitest gerne im Team und schätzt es strukturiert sowie dienstleistungsorientiert zu arbeiten. Bei komplexen Zusammenhängen behaltest du den Überblick und arbeitest dich leicht in neue Technologien ein. Du verfügst über die folgenden oder ähnlichen Kenntnisse:

  • Fundament: Abgeschlossene IT-Ausbildung oder ein Studium in einer technisch relevanten Fachrichtung.
  • System-Know-how: Vertiefte Kenntnisse in der Administration von Windows Clients, sowie Erfahrung mit macOS, iOS, Linux-Knowhow
  • Automatisierung: Praktische Erfahrung im Scripting (Bash, Python); Grundkenntnisse in PowerShell sind ein Plus.
  • Kommunikation: Du schätzt den direkten Austausch mit Usern und kommunizierst sicher in Deutsch und Englisch.
  • Weiterbildung: Du bringst die Bereitschaft mit, dich in dieser spannenden Funktion stetig weiterzubilden.

Project Manager Engineering

Project Manager Engineering

Purpose of the role

As a Project Manager Engineering, you will lead cross-functional product development and engineering projects from concept through implementation, ensuring timely delivery, stakeholder alignment, and innovative solutions that support business and customer objectives.

Responsibilities

  • Lead product development, customization, relocation, and cost-down projects with a strong focus on innovation.
  • Manage cross-functional project teams within a global matrix organization.
  • Oversee end-to-end project execution, ensuring delivery on scope, timeline, quality, and budget.
  • Act as the main interface between project teams and internal/external stakeholders, ensuring effective communication and alignment.
  • Foster a collaborative and innovative team environment that encourages continuous learning and individual contribution.
  • Promote continuous improvement by identifying opportunities to optimize project processes and outcomes.
  • Identify, assess, and mitigate project risks to ensure successful execution.
  • Coordinate technical, supply chain, marketing, and quality activities throughout the project lifecycle.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Engineering (Chemistry, Physics, Mechanical, Electrical, Mechatronics, or a related field).
  • Proven experience as a Project Manager, ideally within a global matrix organization.
  • Experience leading complex product development projects from concept to prototype with minimal supervision.
  • Broad understanding of engineering, supply chain, marketing, and quality processes.
  • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence at all organizational levels.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office, SharePoint, MS365, DevOps, and project management tools.
  • Fluent in English; additional languages are considered an advantage.
  • Experience in the vacuum technology or semiconductor industry is beneficial.

Demand Planner

Main Purpose of the Role

The (Senior) Demand Planner is responsible for creating and maintaining an accurate demand forecast to support business and operational planning. This role combines data analysis with close collaboration across Sales and Business Units to drive consensus and manage market volatility risks. Additionally, you will act as a key bridge between business operations and our technical team to help adopt next-gen digital and AI tools.

 

Key Responsibilities

 

  • Process Ownership: Own and execute the monthly demand planning and forecasting process, consolidating various inputs across sales, baseline data, order books, life-cycle management information, and customer forecasts.
  • S&OP Alignment: Facilitate forecasting meetings across various Business Units and Sales Organizations to drive consensus, and actively contribute to the broader monthly demand review and S&OP cycles.
  • Performance Analytics: Deep-dive into forecast accuracy to identify root causes of deviations and translate data into actionable insights for executive decision-making.
  • Continuous Innovation: Drive continuous improvement in data quality, integrating market intelligence and modern forecasting methodologies to elevate the process.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Act as the business counterpart to our technical team members, collaborating on data quality, KPIs, and the integration of digital tools.

 

Requirements

 

  • Education: University degree (Bachelor's or Master's) in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Engineering, Data Analytics, or a related quantitative field.
  • Experience: 3+ years of relevant experience in demand planning, supply chain orchestration, or a highly analytical business role.
  • Process Know-how: Hands-on experience with baseline forecasting, demand planning cycles, and ERP/MRP environments.
  • Communication: Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear business stories.
  • Languages: Fluent in English (German is a plus).

 

Nice-to-Have

 

  • Experience or strong interest in AI-driven forecasting methodologies, machine learning applications in supply chain, or predictive analytics.
  • Familiarity with modern data stack environments and visualization tools (e.g., PowerBI, Tableau, or collaborating on Python-based prototypes).
  • Background in incorporating external market intelligence or macro data trends into internal baseline forecasts.

job_role: Business & Data Analyst Smart Meter Communication
job_description:
Intelligente Messsysteme sind ein zentrales Rückgrat der modernen Energieversorgung. Mit der flächendeckenden Einführung von Smart Metern und dem Entstehen neuer Energieprodukte am Markt wächst die Menge und Bedeutung von Messdaten rasant. Sie bilden die Grundlage für Abrechnung, Netzplanung, Lastprognosen und regulatorische Anforderungen. Im Team «Smart Meter Communication» sind wir für die stabile, sichere und zuverlässige Kommunikation von rund 400'000 Smart Metern verantwortlich. Diese Stelle wird neu geschaffen, um das Team im Bereich Daten, Analyse und Monitoring gezielt zu verstärken. Du übernimmst eine Schlüsselrolle beim Aufbau eines professionellen Überwachungs- und Monitoring-Systems für unsere Kommunikationsinfrastruktur. Dazu erarbeitest du zunächst die Grundlagen durch strukturierte Prozess- und Datenanalyse und baust mittelfristig Business-Intelligence-Fähigkeiten im Team auf.

Das erwartet dich

  • Analyse, Strukturierung und Dokumentation bestehender Prozesse, Datenflüsse und Schnittstellen entlang der Kommunikationskette gehören zu deinem Verantwortungsbereich
  • Dabei stellst du sicher, dass Dokumentationen stets aktuell und nachvollziehbar bleiben
  • Ein tiefes Verständnis für die Auswertung relevanter Messdaten und Systemparameter erarbeitest du dir kontinuierlich
  • In enger Zusammenarbeit mit verschiedenen internen Teams und Bereichen kommunizierst du Ergebnisse zielgruppengerecht
  • Die Anforderungen des Teams an ein Monitoring-System erfasst du und koordinierst deren Umsetzung
  • Gemeinsam mit dem Team definierst du relevante Kennzahlen und visualisierst diese aussagekräftig
  • Aus Rohdaten leitest du Erkenntnisse ab, identifizierst Verbesserungspotenziale und bringst entsprechende Massnahmen ein

Wir glauben daran, dass Daten so etwas wie der Pulsschlag der CSS sind - heute mehr denn je. Damit datengetriebene Entscheidungen möglich sind, braucht es eine stabile, verlässliche und leistungsfähige Datenbasis. Als Teil des Agile Release Trains (ART) Data & Analytics verantwortet unser DWH-Team das geschäftskritische Data Warehouse der CSS. Hier laufen zentrale Datenströme zusammen, die täglich von zahlreichen Fachbereichen genutzt werden. In unserem Release Train stellen wir den zuverlässigen Betrieb der bestehenden Teradata-Lösung sicher und treiben gleichzeitig die Transformation hin zur modernen Cloud-Datenplattform voran. Bei uns erwarten dich verantwortungsvolle Aufgaben mit grosser Wirkung in einem SAFe-Umfeld, ein erfahrenes Team und eine professionelle IT-Organisation. Wann lernen wir uns kennen?

Product Owner Data Warehouse (DWH) (w/m) 100%

So sieht dein Alltag aus

  • Zum Herzstück: Du übernimmst die fachliche Verantwortung für unser Teradata Data Warehouse und sorgst gemeinsam mit dem Team für dessen Stabilität, Qualität und Unterhalt.
  • Du arbeitest eng mit Fachbereichen, Architekten, Engineers und weiteren Stakeholdern zusammen, priorisierst Anforderungen und unterstützt den Product Manager bei der Definition der Roadmap.
  • Dank deiner Expertise erfasst du die Zusammenhänge über die gesamte Wertschöpfungskette hinweg und kannst Anforderungen von der Quelle bis zur Nutzung im Business fundiert beurteilen.
  • Du bringst deine technische Expertise bei komplexen Fragestellungen rund um Datenintegration und Datenqualität ein und unterstützt das Team bei Bedarf auch operativ.
  • Stillstand gibt es bei uns nicht: Heute verantwortest du unser zentrales DWH als Produkt - perspektivisch wartet auf dich die Übernahme einer PO-Rolle auf unserer modernen Cloud-Datenplattform.
  • Dabei bist du Teil eines Agile Release Trains, der nach dem Lean-Agile-Mindset von SAFe arbeitet und eine Kultur der Zusammenarbeit und kontinuierlichen Verbesserung lebt.

Das bringst du mit

  • Das Wichtigste zuerst: Du verfügst über mehrere Jahre Erfahrung als Product Owner im DWH- oder Datenumfeld und hast Data-Warehouse-Lösungen mit End-to-End-Verantwortung als Produkt gesteuert, weiterentwickelt und erfolgreich im Betrieb begleitet - idealerweise in einem agilen Umfeld.
  • Du verfügst über ein Studium (Uni/FH) in Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder eine vergleichbare Ausbildung mit fundiertem Verständnis von zentralisierten Data-Warehouse-Architekturen und Datenplattformen.
  • Du verstehst sowohl die technische als auch die (finanz-)fachliche Perspektive von Datenprodukten und kannst unterschiedliche Stakeholder erfolgreich zusammenbringen.
  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse in klassischen Data-Warehouse-Technologien gehören zu deinem Profil; Teradata-Erfahrung und ein Hintergrund als Data Engineer mit ausgeprägtem technischem Verständnis sind ein Plus.
  • Du schätzt die Verantwortung für geschäftskritische Systeme und hast Freude daran, ein Data Warehouse nachhaltig und zuverlässig zu betreiben und zu unterhalten. Dabei arbeitest du strukturiert, denkst vernetzt und kommunizierst souverän mit technischen sowie fachlichen Ansprechpartnern.
  • Das Tüpfelchen auf dem i: Kenntnisse in Azure, Databricks oder modernen Cloud-Datenplattformen helfen dir, unsere Transformation aktiv mitzugestalten, werden aber nicht vorausgesetzt.

job_role: Senior Manager – Data Architect – Artificial Intelligence & Data
job_description:
We proudly offer 
  • A safe workplace with zero tolerance for harassment. 
  • Open environments where people are free to be themselves. 
  • An inclusive community that believes in respect and merit.  

Location: Zurich, Basel, Geneva 
Work Pattern Percentage: 80-100% 

You bring ambition, we provide opportunities 

Help our clients navigate the future with confidence and embrace decision making with clear data-driven choices 

Deloitte AI & Data helps clients unlock the power of AI and data to deliver better human-centric solutions that catalyse growth and efficiencies. We enable them to leverage data, analytics, modern platforms, and data science & AI technologies to uncover hidden relationships from vast troves of data, generate insights, and inform their decision-making.  

We help clients transform their business by designing and implementing data solutions to excel in their markets. Our role is to understand their business problems and use data-driven thinking and technology to achieve outcomes that drive their business forward. 

How you can make an impact 

As a Data Architect in our AI & Data team, you will help senior people at our clients to make the right architectural choices and lead the design and implementation of innovative enterprise-wide solutions.  

Your will be able to make significant impact in the following areas: 

  • Lead Enterprise Data Architecture design: help clients modernize complex system landscapes spanning multiple solutions (e.g., MDM, CRM, ERP, Cloud DWH) that need to be integrated. You will work with senior client stakeholders to design enterprise level architecture, pragmatic and enforceable architecture standards, and plan with them the modernization journey.  
  • Lead Cloud Data Platform design & implementation: help clients build scalable, high-performance Cloud data platforms that meet the business needs. Your work will cover data modelling, data integration, transformation pipelines, DevOps, and use of AI to build and operate these platforms smartly. You will make key design decisions and lead onshore, near-/offshore engineering teams developing the solution.  
  • Architect Integrated Solutions: help clients design and build integrations required to run their business processes and deliver data products. This may include integration between Cloud data platform and AI/Gen AI platform or between MDM or CRM systems and Cloud data platform. 
  • Strengthen our market positioning and opportunities funnel: lead our architecture footprint in the market through events, conferences, and discussions with our key clients, internal teams, and alliance partners. 
  • Develop our internal know-how and expertise: mentor junior team members within AI & Data and set strategic focus for expertise building within Switzerland and in relation to near- and offshore teams.   

What We Are Looking For: 

  • Experience: 
    • You have 8 or more years of relevant professional experience in the role of a Data Architect and have led teams that designed and implemented complex cloud-based ecosystems  
    • You have engineering background and multiple hands-on data platform end-to-end implementation experiences as a data engineer in the past (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Databricks, Snowflake)  
    • You are proficient in evaluating and/or taking data architecture decisions, influencing strategic direction and convincing business and IT stakeholders  
    • You are proficient in guiding data engineers with best practices in coding styles and coding quality and are familiar with industrial coding quality tools such as SonarQube as well as AI coding assistants integrated into IDE  
    • You have experience across a range of database types, including structured (relational), semi-structured (NoSQL), and graph-based (knowledge graph) models. 
    • Nice to have: you have experience in packaging data platform solutions into Infrastructure as Code using solutions such as Terraform 
  • Drive and Motivation: 
    • Focused individuals looking to grow their careers on the path of becoming Senior Data Architect within AI & Data consulting 
    • Keen interest and ability to stay on top of technology and architectural trends 
    • Ability to manage multiple activities in a high-paced environment and context switch without losing focus or big picture 
  • Leadership, commercial acumen, and communication skills: 
    • Strong client-facing presence and ability to connect with different senior stakeholders, build relationships, and assess, expand and convert opportunities into projects 
    • Strong abstract reasoning skills to grasp enterprise level, solution level, and operational concepts and how they link together to form a solution 
    • Strong communications skills both oral and written and ability to clearly articulate point of view and resonate with technical and business stakeholders  
    • Strong team leadership skills with ability to partner with externals, estimate and plan the work, and coach team members to deliver optimal client outcomes 
  • Technical Expertise: 
    • Strong skills in modelling methods with experience or certifications from DAMA, TOGAF or equivalent: 
    • Conceptual, logical, and physical data models 
    • Dimensional modelling 
    • Data Vault 
    • Design of normalized and denormalized schemas for analytical and operational workloads and understanding of modelling toolkit 
    • Hands-on experience in designing and implementing solutions on major cloud platforms:  
    • Amazone Web Services (AWS) 
    • Google Cloud Platform (GCP) 
    • Azure 
    • Expertise in architecting modern data platforms (Lakehouse, Medallion, Lambda, event-driven) in Snowflake and/or Databricks, or equivalent 
    • Design E2E architectures that balance performance, cost, and maintainability 
    • Estimate implementation effort and lead delivery teams 
    • Create architecture blueprints and solution design documents 
    • Use of GenAI capabilities to optimize delivery and operation 
    • Data Integration & ETL 
    • Design integration layer architecture from multiple sources (ERP, CRM, operational databases, APIs, files) 
    • Nice to have: Familiarity with ETL/ELT such as Informatica or DBT 
    • Metadata and event-driven architecture: 
    • Expertise in metadata and event-driven architecture with the ability to articulate best practices for capturing metadata within a data pipeline 
    • Ability to leverage metadata to develop event-driven logic within the pipeline for monitoring and alerts 
    • Enterprise Architectures with strong focus of integrating MDM solutions with data platforms and extending data platform with AI Platform components 
    • Programming frameworks and data formats: 
    • Ability to review engineering work done in SQL, Python, Spark 
    • Familiarity with relational and NoSQL databases, knowledge graphs, and big data file formats (Parquet, Iceberg) 
    • Language Proficiency: 
    • Fluent in English; fluency in German and/or French is highly preferred  

Your team 

Become part of a diverse and multinational team of 60+ highly motivated individuals located in our Zurich, Geneva, or Basel offices. We specialise in AI & data – how to structure it, how to source it, how to transform it, how to store it, and how to consume it. Join our fast-paced and fun team, working alongside highly talented colleagues on varied and exciting projects, with access to excellent and continuous learning opportunities. 

Deloitte AI & Data is the leading data & analytics consulting team in Switzerland. You will have a chance to work with outstanding colleagues and have access to variety of clients, vendors, and technologies. Our business is growing fast and there are plenty market opportunities to develop career in the direction that you want. 

We will help you grow both personally and professionally: through our trainings, mentoring programmes, and on-the-job learning, but also by working with clients and vendors across different industries. 

Careers at Deloitte. Choose your impact.  

At Deloitte, your ideas create impact and spark meaningful change for our clients, people and society. Your unique expertise, background and perspective helps us find new ways into the most complex challenges so we can brighten the futures of those our work affects. At Deloitte, you can build a career that inspires and energises you. At Deloitte, you can choose your impact. 

In Switzerland, we provide industry-specific services in the areas of Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal, Strategy, Risk & Transactions Advisory, and Technology & Transformation. With close to 3,000 employees at six locations, we serve companies and organisations of all sizes in all industry sectors. We offer meaningful and challenging work, an inclusive and supportive environment where you can be your best self and where you will never stop growing. You will benefit from flexible and hybrid working conditions, wellbeing and parental programmes as well as many opportunities to connect, collaborate and learn.  

Join us and become part of a global network of like-minded people dedicated to making a difference, whether by addressing climate change or improving 100 million futures by 2030. Want to know more about opportunities at Deloitte? Visit www.apply.deloitte.ch

How to apply  
 
We look forward to hearing from you! Please click on “apply now” to submit a complete application, including CV, cover letter, references and degree certificates.  

If you have any questions, please contact Aarabi at Write an email
    
*We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, ethnicity, religious beliefs, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age or disability, in accordance with applicable law. Research shows that women are less likely to apply for roles unless they match all the criteria; don’t hold yourself back – apply today.  

Deloitte Consulting AG and Deloitte AG are affiliates of Deloitte NSE LLP, a member firm of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee (“DTTL”)

Bachem ist ein börsenkotiertes, auf die Peptid-Chemie fokussiertes Technologie-Unternehmen mit einem umfassenden Leistungsangebot für die Pharma- und Biotechnologie-Industrie. Bachem ist spezialisiert auf die Entwicklung innovativer, effizienter Herstellungsverfahren und die zuverlässige Produktion Peptid-basierter pharmazeutischer Wirkstoffe. Ein umfangreicher Katalog von Biochemikalien für Forschungszwecke und exklusive Kundensynthesen ergänzen das Angebot. Mit Hauptsitz in der Schweiz sowie Niederlassungen in Europa und den USA ist das Unternehmen weltweit tätig und verfügt über die industrieweit grösste Erfahrung und Fachkompetenz. In der Zusammenarbeit mit ihren Kunden legt Bachem grössten Wert auf Qualität, Innovation und Partnerschaft.

IT Service Delivery Manager (a), 80-100%

To drive our continuous organic growth, we are constantly looking for highly qualified professionals. To strengthen our team in Global IT, we are looking for a dedicated IT Service Delivery Manager (a), 80-100%

Your tasks:

  • Own core ITSM processes — Lead Incident, Problem, Configuration and Event Management globally, ensuring SLA compliance and alignment with SOPs/ITIL.
  • Define performance metrics — Set KPIs/SLAs, build dashboards, deliver monthly reports and manage escalations
  • Maintain process documentation — Create and update SOPs, training materials and ensure role clarity with tracked training records
  • Drive Problem Management — Run RCA processes, remediation follow‑up, stakeholder communication and proactive risk identification
  • Manage Known Errors — Maintain Key Issue databases and ensure workarounds are documented and shared
  • Lead CMDB governance — Own CMDB strategy, CI lifecycle accuracy, data models and service dependency mapping
  • Ensure CMDB data quality — Perform audits, reconciliation, issue resolution and integration with change/incident/monitoring processes
  • Optimize CMDB tooling — Enhance CMDB/asset platform usage, automate via discovery tools and report on configuration compliance
  • Run Event Management — Ensure monitoring coverage, correct event filtering/correlation and impact‑based escalation
  • Define event automation — Maintain thresholds, notifications and automated/self‑healing responses

 

Your profile:

  • 7+ years in IT Operations, Service Management or a related technical field, including Service Operations or Service Delivery Management
  • Hands-on experience in Incident, Problem, Configuration and/or Event Management; Major Incident Management is an advantage
  • ITIL Foundation certification required; ITIL Intermediate/Expert an asset.
  • Strong cross-domain technical expertise across infrastructure, applications, databases, and security, with a deep understanding of their interdependencies—including modern workplace devices, application servers, and backend platforms
  • Strong analytical skills to identify patterns, correlations and root causes, with the ability to lead technical RCA calls
  • Excellent communication skills, translating technical detail into business- and user-friendly language
  • Strong facilitation and leadership under pressure, with the ability to work across global teams and manage stakeholders at all levels
  • Experience with monitoring, log analysis and service management platforms (e.g., ServiceNow, LogicMonitor).

 

Our offer:

  • A dynamic and rapidly growing work environment with internal development and training opportunities
  • Flexible working hours with home office days and an option for obtaining additional vacation days through workload reduction
  • Company‑run daycare center on the Bachem Campus in Bubendorf
  • 60% coverage of pension fund contributions by Bachem AG as well as option for extra-mandatory pension provision with our Pension Plan Plus
  • Access to benefits platforms with discounts from external partners
  • Fresh, healthy and varied food in our staff restaurant
  • A wide range of free sports activities on the Bachem Campus

 

Would you like to drive innovation together with us?

We look forward to receiving your complete application documents via our application portal.

Learn more about the Bachem Group and get inspired by our exciting work environment at our location in Bubendorf!

job_role: Senior Identity- und Access Management Engineer (w/m) 80-100%
job_description:
Stabilität und Veränderung - passt das zusammen? Bei der CSS schon. Für die besten IT-Lösungen geben wir alles - und das Besondere: Wir machen fast alles selbst. Wir bauen eine zentrale IAM-Plattform, die Mitarbeitenden, Kundinnen und Kunden, Partnern und Systemen einen sicheren und einheitlichen Zugriff auf unsere Services ermöglicht. Unser IAM entwickelt sich vom reaktiven Verwalter zum proaktiven Business Enabler und unterstützt direkt die strategischen Ziele der CSS.

Als IAM Engineer setzt du "ein Login für alles" in die Praxis um: Du implementierst ein durchgängiges Single Sign-on, verbesserst die User-Experience mit modernen, nahtlosen Login-Prozessen und unterstützt unsere Transformation zum Zero Trust Enterprise aktiv mit. Dich erwarten eine heterogene Systemlandschaft aus Enterprise-Anwendungen und modernen Cloud-Lösungen, viel Gestaltungsspielraum mit grosszügigem Homeoffice-Anteil und Raum für deine Weiterentwicklung. Wann lernen wir uns kennen?

Senior Identity- und Access Management Engineer (w/m) 80-100%

So sieht dein Alltag aus

  • Build & Run der Core IAM Plattformen: Du entwickelst, betreibst und optimierst unsere IAM-Plattform mit Fokus auf Okta, Auth0, Entra ID und Keycloak. Der zentrale Identity-Broker wird dabei zur Drehscheibe, die Anmeldeverfahren vereinheitlicht und Identitäten über alle Kanäle hinweg konsistent bedient.
  • Mehrere IdPs orchestrieren: Du kennst die Stärken verschiedener Identity Provider (Entra ID für das Microsoft- und Cloud-Ökosystem, Okta und Auth0 für SaaS- sowie B2C/B2B-Szenarien, Keycloak für spezifische Applikationen und Self-Hosting) und orchestrierst sie zu einer konsistenten Gesamtlösung - inklusive Federation, SSO und standardisierten Flows (SAML, OIDC, OAuth).
  • Moderne Authentifizierung & Föderation: Die neue Plattform unterstützt von Grund auf moderne Authentifizierungsmethoden (MFA, App-basierte Verfahren, E-ID) und Identitätsföderationen. Du konzipierst und implementierst diese Verfahren und sicherst damit sowohl unsere Cloud-Strategie als auch die Services auf der Integrationsplattform konsequent ab.
  • CI/CD & Automation leben: Du automatisierst Deployments mit Terraform, baust und pflegst CI/CD-Pipelines und stellst sicher, dass unsere IAM-Services reproduzierbar, skalierbar und auditierbar ausgerollt werden.
  • IAM als Business Enabler mitgestalten: Du denkst IAM nicht nur technisch, sondern auch aus Business-Sicht: Du unterstützt digitale Initiativen, reduzierst Reibungsverluste im Zugriff und hilfst mit, unser IAM als Enabler für neue Services und Geschäftsmodelle zu positionieren.
  • IAM-Team mitprägen: Wir etablieren ein zentrales IAM-Team mit klarer Verantwortung für EIAM, CIAM, PIAM und PAM. Du bringst deine Engineering-Kompetenz ein und hilfst mit, eine einheitliche Strategie, Architektur und effiziente Governance zu etablieren.

Das bringst du mit

  • Erfahrung im Identity- & Access Management: Du hast mindestens 5 Jahre Erfahrung im IAM-Bereich, idealerweise in komplexen, hybriden Umgebungen.
  • Know-how in modernen IAM-Tools & IdPs: Du kennst Okta und Auth0 aus der Praxis und hast Erfahrung mit Entra ID (inkl. Conditional Access, MFA, App-Registrierungen) und Keycloak oder vergleichbaren IdP-/IAM-Lösungen. Erfahrung mit NetIQ und NEXIS ist ein Plus.
  • Automation & Engineering-Skills: Du bist sicher im Umgang mit Terraform und CI/CD-Pipelines sowie Scripting (z.B. PowerShell, Python). APIs und Integrationen gehören für dich selbstverständlich zum Engineering-Alltag.
  • Verständnis für IAM-Disziplinen: Du verfügst über fundiertes Wissen in Authentifizierung, Autorisierung sowie Rollen- und Berechtigungsmodellen. PAM und gängige Security-Standards / Zero Trust sind dir bestens vertraut.
  • Arbeitsweise & Mindset: Du hast Freude daran, Dinge voranzutreiben, dein Wissen zu teilen und Verantwortung zu übernehmen. Strukturiertes, selbstständiges Arbeiten und eine hohe Eigeninitiative gehören zu deinen Stärken.

job_role: Data scientist in diagnostic molecular pathology 80-100%
job_description:
The unit of Molecular Pathology at the Department of Pathology and Molecular Pathology at the University Hospital Zurich, is a leading unit for developments in precision medicine including tumor genomics and multi-omics data integration. The diagnostic analyses include numerous modern omics-methods and cutting edge technologies: Whole genome sequencing, bulk RNA sequencing, methylation array, digital pathology, panel sequencing. Our scientists analyze the raw data of these assays to create diagnostic reports, develop new methods and support research projects.
Das Universitätsspital Zürich ist eines der grössten Spitäler in der Schweiz. Unsere über 8'600 Mitarbeitenden und rund 700 Auszubildende setzen sich täglich für das Wohl unserer Patient:innen ein.

Data scientist in diagnostic molecular pathology 80-100%

immediately or upon agreement, limited for 1 year, with the aim of prolonging

Your main tasks

  • Independent analysis, quality control, and interpretation of NGS and multi-omics data in a diagnostic setting
  • Development, validation, and implementation of bioinformatics pipelines for routine molecular diagnostics
  • Immunogenomics analyses with clinically relevant interpretation
  • Application of machine learning / AI methods to omics data, with emphasis on robustness and interpretability
  • Translation of new methods into routine diagnostics, including documentation and validation
  • Preparation of clinical reports in collaboration with pathologists and molecular biologists
  • Support in training medical and laboratory staff in data interpretation and bioinformatics workflows

Your profile

  • MSc or PhD in Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, Medical Informatics, or a related field
  • Strong experience in NGS/omics data analysis in an applied setting
  • Proficiency in Python and/or R (Bioconductor)
  • Strong understanding of molecular biology and genetics, with the ability to interpret data in a biological and clinical context
  • Solid understanding of statistics and machine learning
  • Experience with workflow management systems (Nextflow, Snakemake) and containerization (Docker/Singularity)
  • Excellent analytical, communication, and writing skills
  • English required, German is an advantage

Preferred qualifications: experience in cancer genomics, immunogenomics, transcriptomics or epigenetics, metagenomics, AI-based image analysis, data management/structured reporting, integrative multi-omics, or work in a clinical/medical environment

Unsere Benefits

We offer an interdisciplinary position connecting interpretation and reporting of molecular diagnostic big data with clinical science at the Department of Pathology and Molecular Pathology, University Hospital Zurich within a strong collaborative framework including experts at other leading Swiss Institutions. In Zurich, you will be immersed in a dynamic, open and motivating scientific and academic environment. We are supported by intra- and extramural funding and are involved in several high-visibility national and international collaborations.
Universitätsspital Zürich
8091 Zürich

Du willst nicht nur Features bauen, sondern eine digitale Plattform weiterentwickeln, die hunderttausende Menschen nutzen? Dann könnte das hier spannend für dich sein.

Als Senior Frontend Engineer bist du Teil eines agilen Entwicklungsteams und entwickelst unsere Webseite mit Self-Service weiter. Die Webseite bietet unseren Kundinnen und Kunden einen einfachen Zugriff auf Mutationen, Anträge und Anfragen. Ausserdem stellt dein Team eine Plattform bereit, mit der E-Mail-Templates erstellt und zielgerichtete Mailings versendet werden.

Dich erwartet ein technisch vielseitiges Umfeld mit einem modernen Technologie-Stack. Flexible Arbeitszeiten, Innovation Days und einen ausgewogenen Homeoffice-Anteil sorgen dafür, dass auch das Drumherum stimmt. Klingt gut? Dann freuen wir uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen.

Senior Frontend Engineer Web (w/m) 80-100%

So sieht dein Alltag aus

  • Zum Herzstück: Du entwickelst das Frontend unserer Webseite weiter und arbeitest mit einem breiten Technologie-Stack von Standardtechnologien wie CSS/SCSS, HTML, TypeScript über Vue.js bis hin zu Storybook. Alles ist integriert in unser Content Management System AEM as a Cloud Service.
  • Im Bereich UI/UX arbeitest du mit Figma, hältst die Designs up-to-date und entwickelst diese kontinuierlich weiter.
  • Du bindest Schnittstellen an das Frontend an, stellst Unit und UI Testing sicher, betreust Pipelines und wirkst aktiv an unserer Cloud-Infrastruktur auf Azure mit.
  • Du erarbeitest im Team architekturkonforme Lösungen und setzt sie um. Zudem baust du automatisierte Tests auf und führst Code-Reviews durch.
  • You build it, you run it! Als DevOps- und Scrum-Teammitglied bist du verantwortlich für die Software, von der Konzeption bis zum Betrieb.
  • Dabei bist du Teil eines Agile Release Trains, der nach dem Lean-Agile-Mindset von SAFe arbeitet und eine Kultur der Zusammenarbeit und kontinuierlichen Verbesserung lebt.

Das bringst du mit

  • Das Wichtigste zuerst: Dein Herz schlägt für Frontend-Entwicklung, DevOps und du kannst dich für UI/UX begeistern. Du hast mindestens fünf Jahre Erfahrung in der agilen Softwareentwicklung mit aktuellen Frontend-Technologien und Spass an komplexen Fragestellungen.
  • Eine Informatikausbildung oder ein Studium im Bereich der Softwareentwicklung - egal ob klassisch oder mit Umwegen, wir sind offen für deinen individuellen Karriereweg. 
  • Du bist vertraut mit Technologien wie CSS/SCSS, HTML, Typescript, Figma, und bringst idealerweise auch Erfahrung im Einsatz von AI-Tools mit.

Development Engineer Sensor Protection Advanced Sensors 

 

We at VAT Group AG, headquartered in Switzerland - Haag, invite a talented and passionate Development Engineer Corrosion Protection to join our innovative development environment.
You will be responsible for strengthening our competencies in the field of vacuum sensor protection against corrosive gases and material deposition in semiconductor processes. In this role, you will contribute to accelerating the time-to-market of our advanced sensor products by developing and implementing effective protection concepts and solutions. Through your expertise, you will support the continuous advancement of our sensor technologies and help ensure reliable performance in demanding semiconductor manufacturing environments. 

 

WHAT WE OFFER

 

  • Join a global leader at the forefront of semiconductor innovation, we are a globally leading company in the semiconductor industry

  • A technologically advanced and innovative working environment with Modern Laboratory and testing facilities

  • Thrive in a collaborative, innovative environment that celebrates bold ideas and outstanding execution — where your potential knows no bounds

  • Experience the latest trends and technologies of the semiconductor business and collaborate with a highend Development Team
  • Transfer your innovative ideas into new products to further strengthen the success of VAT

  • Enjoy an exceptional working environment with great amenities at our VAT Group offices in the Swiss canton of St. Gallen

  • Receive competitive compensation and benefits aligned with your expertise and contributions

 

YOUR CHALLENGES 

 

  • Leading the development of advanced sensor protection concepts and technologies
  • Investigation and testing of different materials, coatings and methods
  • Evaluation and application of accelerated testing procedures and setups
  • Development and characterization of scalable material concepts for new generation, industrial sensor assemblies
  • Collaboration with industrial and academic partners
  • Joining a multi-location R&D organization (Haag, Zürich), some work-related travel is required (max. 20%)

 

YOUR COMPETENCIES

  • MSc or PhD degree in material science, physics, chemistry or related field
  • 5+ years of industry experience on material-related topics 
  • Work experience in vacuum technology and/or semiconductor industry
  • Negotiating skills in English and German (fluent in spoken and writing)
  • In-depth understanding of semiconductor processes is beneficial
  • You are used to working independently, proactively and in an organized manner
  • You have a strong ability to work in a team and care about interdisciplinary interface functions

Die Kernaufgabe des Registers besteht darin, die in der Bevölkerung der Kantone ZH, ZG, SH und SZ aufgetretenen Krebsneuerkrankungen zu erfassen und damit die Krebsbelastung systematisch zu dokumentieren. Dieses Monitoring bildet eine zentrale Grundlage für Forschung, Prävention und eine kontinuierliche Verbesserung der Versorgung.
Das Universitätsspital Zürich ist eines der grössten Spitäler in der Schweiz. Unsere über 8'600 Mitarbeitenden und rund 700 Auszubildende setzen sich täglich für das Wohl unserer Patient:innen ein.

Stellvertretende Leitung des Krebsregisters 80-100%

per sofort oder nach Vereinbarung

Deine Aufgaben - vielseitig und bedeutungsvoll

  • Du codierst bereits in der Datenbank erfasste Krebsfälle nach internationalen Standards (ICD-O, TNM) und dokumentierst Therapien sowie medizinische Follow-ups.
  • Als kompetente Ansprechperson unterstützt du Registrant:innen bei Fragen rund um Registierung und Codierung.
  • Du sicherst die Datenqualität durch sorgfältige Kontrollen und kontinuierliche Überprüfung der Vollständigkeit.
  • Du koordinierst Registrierungs- und Codierungsprozesse in enger Abstimmung mit der Leitung und unter Einhaltung der Vorgaben des NKRS.
  • Du arbeitest eng mit meldepflichtigen Ärzt:innen, Spitälern und Labors zusammen.
  • Du arbeitest in Forschungsprojekten des Registers mit internen und externen Partnern mit.
Als stellvertretende Leitung nimmst du zudem folgende Aufgaben wahr:
  • Du begleitest neue Mitarbeitende bei der Einarbeitung und förderst den Wissenstransfer im Team.
  • Du organisierst und erarbeitest Weiterbildungen aktiv mit.
  • In Zusammenarbeit mit der Krebsregisterleitung führst du Rekrutierungs- und Jahresgespräche.

Dein Profil

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium in Medizin, Public Health, Epidemiologie oder Lebenswissenschaften
  • Mehrjährige Erfahrung in der Tumorkodierung sowie fundierte Kenntnisse der medizinischen Terminologie
  • Verständnis für epidemiologische Zusammenhänge und Krebsregistrierung
  • Sicherer Umgang mit gängigen IT-Anwendungen (insbesondere Microsoft Office) sowie Erfahrung mit Datenbanken
  • Strukturierte, sorgfältige und eigenständige Arbeitsweise
